questions.question: Define the Data Encryption Standard (DES) and its significance in the evolution of encryption algorithms.
keyPoints: DES is a symmetric-key block cipher
First encryption algorithm approved by NIST

questions.question: Explain briefly how DES works, including the use of a 56-bit key and 64-bit block size.
keyPoints: Three main stages: key expansion, encryption, and decryption
64-bit block size is divided into two halves for encryption

questions.question: Compare and contrast DES with modern encryption algorithms like AES, including key length, block size, and security.
keyPoints: DES has a shorter key length (56 bits) compared to AES (128, 192, or 256 bits)
DES has a smaller block size (64 bits) compared to AES (128 bits)
DES is considered insecure due to its weaknesses, while AES is considered secure

questions.question: Analyze the security of DES and explain why it is no longer considered secure for modern applications.
keyPoints: Weaknesses of DES include short key length, weak S-boxes, and small block size
Vulnerable to attacks such as brute-force, differential cryptanalysis, and linear cryptanalysis
No longer considered secure due to its weaknesses and the availability of more secure algorithms like AES

questions.question: Describe the different types of attacks that can be launched against DES, including brute-force, differential cryptanalysis, and linear cryptanalysis.
keyPoints: Brute-force attack involves trying all possible keys
Differential cryptanalysis involves analyzing the differences between ciphertexts
Linear cryptanalysis involves analyzing the linear relationships between plaintext and ciphertext
